title: glechoma racemosa
intro: Glechoma racemosa, also known as Creeping Thyme or Creeping Jenny, is a low-growing, perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Glechoma racemosa include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. It is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
